Each scope maintains its own set of keys, independent of\n76\t    keys in parent or sibling scopes.\n77\t    \"\"\"\n78\t    indent_level: int\n79\t    start_line: int\n80\t    parent_key: Args:\n91\t            key: The key to add\n92\t\n93\t        Returns:\n94\t            True if the key already exists in this scope (duplicate), False otherwise\n95\t        \"\"\"\n96\t        if key in self.keys:\n97\t            return True  # Already exists (duplicate)\n98\t        self.keys.add(key)\n99\t        return False  # Successfully added (not a duplicate)\n100\t\n101\t    def )\n130\t\n131\t\n132\tclass ScopeStack:\n133\t    \"\"\"\n134\t    Hierarchical stack of active scopes during YAML parsing.\n135\t\n136\t    The ScopeStack maintains the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes during parsing.\n137\t    As the parser encounters different indentation levels, it enters and exits scopes,\n138\t    ensuring that duplicate key detection only considers keys within the same       self.actual_indent: int = 0  # Tracks actual line indent (including indent-only transitions)\n154\t\n155\t    def depth(self) -> int:\n156\t        \"\"\"Get the number of active scopes in the       path_parts = []\n185\t        for scope in self.scopes:\n186\t            if scope.parent_key:\n187\t                path_parts.append(scope.parent_key)\n188\t        return \".\".join(path_parts)\n189\t\n190\t    def enter_scope(self, indent_level: int, line: int, parent_key: Optional[str] = None):\n191\t        \"\"\"\n192\t        Enter a new scope (when indent increases).\n193\t\n194\t        Args:\n195\t            indent_level: The new indentation level\n196\t            line: The line number where this scope starts (1-indexed)\n197\t            parent_key: Optional parent key that created this scope\n198\t        \"\"\"\n199\t        # Check if we already have a scope at this level\n200\t        existing = )\n209\t            fresh_scope.is_flow_style = existing.is_flow_style\n210\t\n211\t            # Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n212\t            self.scopes = [s for s in self.scopes if create duplicate scope\n216\t            # This happens when root-level keys create scopes\n217\t            # Just update the existing scope's parent_key if needed\n218\t            if )\n228\t            self.scopes.append(new_scope)\n229\t\n230\t    def enter_sequence_scope(self, indent_level: int, line: int):\n231\t        \"\"\"\n232\t        Enter a sequence context (when we   # Always create a new scope for each sequence item\n242\t        self.sequence_item_counter += 1\n243\t        new_scope = Scope(\n244\t            indent_level=indent_level,\n245\t            start_line=line,\n246\t            parent_key=None\n247\t        )\n248\t        new_scope.in_sequence_context = True\n249\t        new_scope.sequence_item_id = self.sequence_item_counter\n250\t        self.scopes.append(new_scope)\n251\t\n252\t    def exit_to_scope(self, target_indent: int):\n253\t        \"\"\"\n254\t        Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases).\n255\t\n256\t        Args:\n257\t            target_indent: The indentation level to exit to\n258\t        \"\"\"\n259\t        if target_indent > self.current_indent():\n260\t            # Can't exit to a deeper level\n261\t            return\n262\t\n263\t        # Remove all scopes deeper than target\n264\t        self.scopes = [s for s in self.scopes if s.indent_level <= target_indent]\n265\t\n266\t        # Ensure we have a scope at the target level\n267\t        if not self.scopes:\n268\t            # Create fallback scope if stack would be empty\n269\t            self.scopes.append(Scope(indent_level=0, start_line=0, parent_key=None))\n270\t        elif not any(s.indent_level == target_indent for s in self.scopes):\n271\t            # No exact match - create fallback\n272\t            fallback = Scope(indent_level=target_indent, start_line=0, parent_key=None)\n273\t            self.scopes.append(fallback)\n274\t\n275\t    def exit_one_level(self) -> bool:\n276\t        \"\"\"\n277\t        Exit one level to immediate parent scope.\n278\t\n279\t        Returns:\n280\t            True if successfully exited to parent, False if already at root scope\n281\t        \"\"\"\n282\t        if self.depth() <= 1:\n283\t            return False\n284\t\n285\t        current_indent = self.current_indent()\n286\t\n287\t        # Find the parent scope's indent level\n288\t        if self.depth() >= 2:\n289\t            parent_indent = self.scopes[-2].indent_level\n290\t        else:\n291\t            parent_indent = 0\n292\t\n293\t        self.exit_to_scope(parent_indent)\n294\t        return True\n295\t\n296\t    def scope_exit_to_level(self, target_depth: int) -> bool:\n297\t        \"\"\"\n298\t        Exit to a specific scope depth level (grandparent or any ancestor).\n299\t\n300\t        This allows exiting multiple levels at once to reach any valid ancestor scope.\n301\t        For example, from depth 5 you can exit directly to depth 2 (skpping depths 4 and 3).\n302\t\n303\t        Args:\n304\t            target_depth: The target depth level to exit to (1-indexed, where 1 is root scope).\n305\t                          Must be a valid ancestor depth between 1 and current depth.\n306\t\n307\t        Returns:\n308\t            True if successfully exited to target depth\n309\t\n310\t        Raises:\n311\t            ValueError: If target depth is invalid (<= 0, > current depth, or not an ancestor)\n312\t        \"\"\"\n313\t        if target_depth <= 0:\n314\t            raise ValueError(f\"Target depth must be positive (got {target_depth})\")\n315\t\n316\t        current_depth = self.depth()\n317\t\n318\t        if target_depth > current_depth:\n319\t            raise ValueError(\n320\t                f\"Cannot exit to depth {target_depth} (current depth is {current_depth})\"\n321\t            )\n322\t\n323\t        if target_depth == current_depth:\n324\t            # Already at target depth - no action needed\n325\t            return True\n326\t\n327\t        # Convert to 0-indexed and get the target scope\n328\t        target_index = target_depth - 1\n329\t\n330\t        if target_index >= len(self.scopes):\n331\t            raise ValueError(\n332\t                f\"Invalid target depth {target_depth} (no scope at that level)\"\n333\t            )\n334\t\n335\t        # Get the target scope and its indent level\n336\t        target_scope = self.scopes[target_index]\n337\t        target_indent = target_scope.indent_level\n338\t\n339\t        # Exit to that scope's indent level\n340\t        self.exit_to_scope(target_indent)\n341\t\n342\t        return True\n343\t\n344\t    def contains_key(self, key: str) -> bool:\n345\t        \"\"\"Check if current scope contains a key.\"\"\"\n346\t        if not self.scopes:\n347\t            return False\n348\t        return self.scopes[-1].contains_key(key)\n349\t\n350\t    def contains_key_in_any_scope(self, key: str) -> bool:\n351\t        \"\"\"Check if any scope in the hierarchy contains a key.\"\"\"\n352\t        return any(scope.contains_key(key) for scope in self.scopes)\n353\t\n354\t    def add_key(self, key: str, line: int) -> None:\n355\t        \"\"\"\n356\t        Add a key to current scope.\n357\t\n358\t        Args:\n359\t            key: The key to add\n360\t            line: The line number where the key appears (1-indexed)\n361\t\n362\t        Raises:\n363\t            DuplicateKeyError: If the key already exists in the current scope\n364\t        \"\"\"\n365\t        if self.contains_key(key):\n366\t            scope = self.current_scope()\n367\t            raise DuplicateKeyError(\n368\t                key=key,\n369\t                scope_path=self.get_scope_path(),\n370\t                first_line=scope.start_line,\n371\t                duplicate_line=line\n372\t            )\n373\t        self.scopes[-1].add_key(key)\n374\t\n375\t    def in_sequence_context(self) -> bool:\n376\t        \"\"\"Check if we're in a sequence context.\"\"\"\n377\t        if not self.scopes:\n378\t            return False\n379\t        return self.scopes[-1].in_sequence_context\n380\t\n381\t    def record_indent_transition(self, line_number: int, new_indent: int,\n382\t                                 has_key: bool, raw_line: str):\n383\t        \"\"\"\n384\t        Record an indent transition.\n385\t\n386\t        Args:\n387\t            line_number: The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n388\t            new_indent: The new indentation level\n389\t            has_key: Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\n390\t            raw_line: The raw line content (for debugging)\n391\t        \"\"\"\n392\t        # Always update actual_indent to track the current line's indentation\n393\t        # This maintains consistency even for indent-only transitions\n394\t        self.actual_indent = new_indent\n395\t\n396\t        if new_indent != self.last_indent:\n397\t            old_indent = self.last_indent\n398\t            line_classification = self._classify_line_type(raw_line)\n399\t\n400\t            transition = IndentTransition(\n401\t                line_number=line_number,\n402\t                from_indent=old_indent,\n403\t                to_indent=new_indent,\n404\t                has_key=has_key,\n405\t                raw_line=raw_line,\n406\t                line_classification=line_classification,\n407\t                transition_type=self._classify_transition(old_indent, new_indent)\n408\t            )\n409\t            self.indent_transitions.append(transition)\n410\t            self.last_indent = new_indent\n411\t\n412\t    def _classify_transition(self, from_indent: int, to_indent: int) -> IndentTransitionType:\n413\t        \"\"\"Classify an indent transition.\"\"\"\n414\t        if to_indent > from_indent:\n415\t            return IndentTransitionType.ENTER_SCOPE\n416\t        elif to_indent < from_indent:\n417\t            return IndentTransitionType.EXIT_SCOPE\n418\t        else:\n419\t            return IndentTransitionType.SAME_LEVEL\n420\t\n421\t    def _classify_line_type(self, line: str) -> LineClassification:\n422\t        \"\"\"Classify a YAML line as key-bearing or indent-only.\"\"\"\n423\t        trimmed = line.strip()\n424\t\n425\t        if not trimmed:\n426\t            return LineClassification.EMPTY\n427\t\n428\t        # Check if line has a key token\n429\t        if self._extract_key_context(line):\n430\t            return LineClassification.KEY_BEARING\n431\t        else:\n432\t            return LineClassification.INDENT_ONLY\n433\t\n434\t    def _has_key_token(self, line: str) -> bool:\n435\t        \"\"\"\n436\t        Detect if a line contains a key token (colon that starts a key-value pair).\n437\t\n438\t        A key token is a colon that:\n439\t        - Is not inside quotes (single or double)\n440\t        - Is not inside a flow collection ({ } or [ ])\n441\t        - Is not part of a block scalar indicator (| or >)\n442\t        - Is not in a comment (after #)\n443\t        - Has a valid key before it\n444\t\n445\t        Args:\n446\t            line: The line to check\n447\t\n448\t        Returns:\n449\t            True if the line contains a key token, False otherwise\n450\t        \"\"\"\n451\t        trimmed = line.strip()\n452\t\n453\t        # Skip empty lines and comments\n454\t        if not trimmed or trimmed.startswith('#'):\n455\t            return False\n456\t\n457\t        # Check for block scalar indicators at the start (after stripping sequence dash)\n458\t        check_line = trimmed\n459\t        if check_line.startswith('- '):\n460\t            check_line = check_line[2:].strip()\n461\t        elif check_line.startswith('-') and len(check_line) > 1:\n462\t            check_line = check_line[1:].strip()\n463\t\n464\t        if check_line.startswith('|') or check_line.startswith('>'):\n465\t            return False\n466\t\n467\t        # Track state while scanning for colon\n468\t        in_single_quote = False\n469\t        in_double_quote = False\n470\t        flow_depth = 0  # Depth of nested {} or []\n471\t\n472\t        for i, char in enumerate(trimmed):\n473\t            # Handle quote state changes\n474\t            if char == '\"' and not in_single_quote:\n475\t                # Toggle double quote (unless escaped)\n476\t                if i == 0 or trimmed[i - 1] != '\\\\':\n477\t                    in_double_quote = not in_double_quote\n478\t            elif char == \"'\" and not in_double_quote:\n479\t                in_single_quote = not in_single_quote\n480\t\n481\t            # Track flow collection depth (only outside quotes)\n482\t            if not in_single_quote and not in_double_quote:\n483\t                if char == '#':\n484\t                    # Rest of line is a comment - stop looking\n485\t                    break\n486\t                elif char in '{[':\n487\t                    flow_depth += 1\n488\t                elif char in '}]':\n489\t                    flow_depth = max(0, flow_depth - 1)\n490\t\n491\t            # Check for colon as key token\n492\t            if char == ':' and not in_single_quote and not in_double_quote and flow_depth == 0:\n493\t                # Check if this could be a key token\n494\t                key_part = trimmed[:i].strip()\n495\t\n496\t                # Validate the key\n497\t                if self._is_valid_key(key_part):\n498\t                    # Check what comes after the colon\n499\t                    if i + 1 >= len(trimmed):\n500\t                        # Colon at end of line - this is a key token (parent mapping)\n501\t                        return True\n502\t                    else:\n503\t                        next_char = trimmed[i + 1]\n504\t                        # Colon followed by whitespace or end of line\n505\t                        if next_char.isspace() or next_char == '#':\n506\t                            return True\n507\t\n508\t        return False\n509\t\n510\t    def _is_valid_key(self, key_part: str) -> bool:\n511\t        \"\"\"\n512\t        Check if a key part is a valid YAML key.\n513\t\n514\t        Args:\n515\t            key_part: The text before a colon\n516\t\n517\t        Returns:\n518\t            True if this is a valid key, False otherwise\n519\t        \"\"\"\n520\t        if not key_part:\n521\t            return False\n522\t\n523\t        key = key_part\n524\t\n525\t        # Remove sequence dash prefix if present\n526\t        if key.startswith(\"- \"):\n527\t            key = key[2:].strip()\n528\t        elif key.startswith('-'):\n529\t            # Handle both single dash and dash followed by non-space\n530\t            key = key[1:].strip()\n531\t\n532\t        # Key must not be empty after stripping dash\n533\t        if not key:\n534\t            return False\n535\t\n536\t        # Key must not contain flow collection markers\n537\t        if any(c in key for c in ['{', '}', '[', ']']):\n538\t            return False\n539\t\n540\t        # Key must not contain comment or block scalar markers\n541\t        if any(c in key for c in ['#', '|', '>']):\n542\t            return False\n543\t\n544\t        # Key must not be just special characters\n545\t        if not any(c.isalnum() or c in '_-.' for c in key):\n546\t            return False\n547\t\n548\t        return True\n549\t\n550\t    def _extract_key_context(self, line: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:\n551\t        \"\"\"\n552\t        Extract key context from a line.\n553\t\n554\t        Returns:\n555\t            Dict with key context info, or None if no key found\n556\t        \"\"\"\n557\t        trimmed = line.strip()\n558\t\n559\t        # Find colon position using improved key token detection\n560\t        if not self._has_key_token(line):\n561\t            return None\n562\t\n563\t        # Find the actual colon position\n564\t        colon_pos = trimmed.find(':')\n565\t        if colon_pos == -1:\n566\t            return None\n567\t\n568\t        key_part = trimmed[:colon_pos]\n569\t        after_colon = trimmed[colon_pos + 1:]\n570\t\n571\t        # Strip sequence dash from key if present\n572\t        key = key_part.strip()\n573\t        if key.startswith(\"- \"):\n574\t            key = key[2:].strip()\n575\t        elif key.startswith('-') and len(key) > 1:\n576\t            key = key[1:].strip()\n577\t\n578\t        if not key:\n579\t            return None\n580\t\n581\t        # Classify based on what comes after the colon\n582\t        if after_colon.strip():\n583\t            return {\n584\t                'type': 'inline_scalar',\n585\t                'key': key,\n586\t                'value': after_colon.strip()\n587\t            }\n588\t        else:\n589\t            return {\n590\t                'type': 'parent_mapping',\n591\t                'key': key\n592\t            }\n593\t\n594\t    def get_indent_transitions(self) -> List[IndentTransition]:\n595\t        \"\"\"Get all recorded indent transitions.\"\"\"\n596\t        return self.indent_transitions.copy()\n597\t\n598\t    def clear_indent_transitions(self):\n599\t        \"\"\"Clear all indent transitions.\"\"\"\n600\t        self.indent_transitions.clear()\n601\t        self.last_indent = 0\n602\t\n603\t    def reset(self):\n604\t        \"\"\"Clear all scopes and reset to root.\"\"\"\n605\t        self.scopes = [Scope(indent_level=0, start_line=0, parent_key=None)]\n606\t        self.clear_indent_transitions()\n607\t\n608\t\n609\tclass YAMLParser:\n610\t    \"\"\"\n611\t    YAML parser with safe_load wrapper, error handling, and scope depth tracking.\n612\t\n613\t    Uses yaml.safe_load() to safely parse YAML files without\n614\t    executing arbitrary Python objects, and provides scope tracking\n615\t    for hierarchical key management.\n616\t    \"\"\"\n617\t\n618\t    def __init__(self, enable_scope_tracking: bool = False, base_indent: int = 2):\n619\t        \"\"\"\n620\t        Initialize the YAML parser.\n621\t\n622\t        Args:\n623\t            enable_scope_tracking: Whether to enable scope depth tracking during parsing\n624\t            base_indent: The base indentation size for scope tracking (usually 2 or 4)\n625\t        \"\"\"\n626\t        self.yaml = None\n627\t        self._import_yaml()\n628\t\n629\t        # Scope tracking state\n630\t        self.enable_scope_tracking = enable_scope_tracking\n631\t        self.scope_stack: Optional[ScopeStack] = None\n632\t        self.base_indent = base_indent\n633\t        self.scope_depth: int = 0\n634\t        self.current_line_type: Optional[LineClassification] = None\n635\t        self.current_line_number: int = 0\n636\t\n637\t    def _import_yaml(self) -> None:\n638\t        \"\"\"\n639\t        Import PyYAML module with fallback handling.\n640\t\n641\t        Raises:\n642\t            RuntimeError: If PyYAML is not available\n643\t        \"\"\"\n644\t        try:\n645\t            import yaml\n646\t            self.yaml = yaml\n647\t        except ImportError:\n648\t            raise RuntimeError(\n649\t                \"PyYAML is required but not available. \"\n650\t                \"Install it via: pip install pyyaml\"\n651\t            )\n652\t\n653\t    def parse_string(self, yaml_content: str) -> ParseResult:\n654\t        \"\"\"\n655\t        Parse YAML from a string.\n656\t\n657\t        Args:\n658\t            yaml_content: String containing YAML content\n659\t\n660\t        Returns:\n661\t            ParseResult with status, data, and error fields\n662\t        \"\"\"\n663\t        if not self.yaml:\n664\t            return ParseResult.make_error('PyYAML not available')\n665\t\n666\t        if not yaml_content or not yaml_content.strip():\n667\t            return ParseResult.make_error('Empty YAML content')\n668\t\n669\t        try:\n670\t            data = self.yaml.safe_load(yaml_content)\n671\t            return ParseResult.success(data)\n672\t        except self.yaml.YAMLError as e:\n673\t            error_msg = self._format_yaml_error(str(e))\n674\t            return ParseResult.make_error(error_msg)\n675\t        except Exception as e:\n676\t            return ParseResult.make_error(f'Unexpected error: {str(e)}')\n677\t\n678\t    def parse_file(self, filepath: str) -> ParseResult:\n679\t        \"\"\"\n680\t        Parse YAML from a file.\n681\t\n682\t        Args:\n683\t            filepath: Path to the YAML file\n684\t\n685\t        Returns:\n686\t            ParseResult with status, data, and error fields\n687\t        \"\"\"\n688\t        path = Path(filepath)\n689\t\n690\t        # Check if file exists\n691\t        if not path.exists():\n692\t            return ParseResult.make_error(f'File not found: {filepath}')\n693\t\n694\t        # Check if it's a file (not directory)\n695\t        if not path.is_file():\n696\t            return ParseResult.make_error(f'Path is not a file: {filepath}')\n697\t\n698\t        try:\n699\t            with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n700\t                content = f.read()\n701\t\n702\t            return self.parse_string(content)\n703\t\n704\t        except FileNotFoundError:\n705\t            return ParseResult.make_error(f'File not found: {filepath}')\n706\t        except PermissionError:\n707\t            return ParseResult.make_error(f'Permission denied: {filepath}')\n708\t        except UnicodeDecodeError as e:\n709\t            return ParseResult.make_error(f'Encoding error reading file: {str(e)}')\n710\t        except Exception as e:\n711\t            return ParseResult.make_error(f'Error reading file: {str(e)}')\n712\t\n713\t    def _format_yaml_error(self, error_message: str) -> str:\n714\t        \"\"\"\n715\t        Format YAML error message for better readability.\n716\t\n717\t        Args:\n718\t            error_message: Raw error message from PyYAML\n719\t\n720\t        Returns:\n721\t            Formatted error message\n722\t        \"\"\"\n723\t        # Clean up common PyYAML error patterns\n724\t        error_message = error_message.strip()\n725\t\n726\t        # Add context for common errors\n727\t        if 'could not find expected' in error_message.lower():\n728\t            return f\"YAML syntax error: {error_message}. Check indentation and structure.\"\n729\t        elif 'mapping values are not allowed here' in error_message.lower():\n730\t            return f\"YAML structure error: {error_message}. Check colons and indentation.\"\n731\t        elif 'duplicate key' in error_message.lower():\n732\t            return f\"YAML validation error: {error_message}\"\n733\t\n734\t        return f\"YAML parsing error: {error_message}\"\n735\t\n736\t    # Scope tracking methods\n737\t\n738\t    def _init_scope_tracking(self):\n739\t        \"\"\"Initialize scope tracking for a new parse operation.\"\"\"\n740\t        if self.enable_scope_tracking:\n741\t            self.scope_stack = ScopeStack(base_indent=self.base_indent)\n742\t            self.scope_depth = 0\n743\t            self.current_line_type = None\n744\t            self.current_line_number = 0\n745\t        else:\n746\t            self.scope_stack = None\n747\t            self.scope_depth = 0\n748\t            self.current_line_type = None\n749\t            self.current_line_number = 0\n750\t\n751\t    def get_scope_depth(self) -> int:\n752\t        \"\"\"\n753\t        Get the current scope depth during parsing.\n754\t\n755\t        Returns:\n756\t            The current nesting depth (includes root scope as depth 1)\n757\t            Returns 0 if scope tracking is not enabled.\n758\t        \"\"\"\n759\t        if self.scope_stack:\n760\t            return self.scope_stack.depth()  # Include all scopes including root\n761\t        return 0\n762\t\n763\t    def get_scope_path(self) -> str:\n764\t        \"\"\"\n765\t        Get the current scope path as a dot-separated string.\n766\t\n767\t        Returns:\n768\t            A string like \"services.web.database\" representing the scope hierarchy.\n769\t            Returns empty string if scope tracking is not enabled.\n770\t        \"\"\"\n771\t        if self.scope_stack:\n772\t            return self.scope_stack.get_scope_path()\n773\t        return \"\"\n774\t\n775\t    def get_parent_scope(self) -> Optional[str]:\n776\t        \"\"\"\n777\t        Get the parent scope reference.\n778\t\n779\t        Returns:\n780\t            The parent scope key if available, None otherwise.\n781\t        \"\"\"\n782\t        if self.scope_stack and self.scope_stack.depth() > 1:\n783\t            # Get the second-to-last scope's parent_key (current scope's parent)\n784\t            return self.scope_stack.scopes[-2].parent_key if len(self.scope_stack.scopes) > 1 else None\n785\t        return None\n786\t\n787\t    def get_scope_stack(self) -> Optional[ScopeStack]:\n788\t        \"\"\"\n789\t        Get the current scope stack.\n790\t\n791\t        Returns:\n792\t            The ScopeStack instance if scope tracking is enabled, None otherwise.\n793\t        \"\"\"\n794\t        return self.scope_stack\n795\t\n796\t    def parse_with_scope_tracking(self, yaml_content: str) -> ParseResult:\n797\t        \"\"\"\n798\t        Parse YAML content with scope depth tracking enabled.\n799\t\n800\t        This method parses the YAML content line-by-line to track scope depth\n801\t        and hierarchy during parsing. It returns both the parsed data and\n802\t        scope tracking information.\n803\t\n804\t        Args:\n805\t            yaml_content: String containing YAML content\n806\t\n807\t        Returns:\n808\t            ParseResult with status, data, and error fields. If successful,\n809\t            the parser's scope tracking state will be populated.\n810\t        \"\"\"\n811\t        if not self.yaml:\n812\t            return ParseResult.make_error('PyYAML not available')\n813\t\n814\t        if not yaml_content or not yaml_content.strip():\n815\t            return ParseResult.make_error('Empty YAML content')\n816\t\n817\t        # Initialize scope tracking\n818\t        original_setting = self.enable_scope_tracking\n819\t        self.enable_scope_tracking = True\n820\t        self._init_scope_tracking()\n821\t\n822\t        try:\n823\t            # First, do the regular parse\n824\t            data = self.yaml.safe_load(yaml_content)\n825\t\n826\t            # Then, track scopes by processing line by line\n827\t            self._track_scopes_from_yaml(yaml_content)\n828\t\n829\t            return ParseResult.success(data)\n830\t        except self.yaml.YAMLError as e:\n831\t            error_msg = self._format_yaml_error(str(e))\n832\t            return ParseResult.make_error(error_msg)\n833\t        except Exception as e:\n834\t            error_msg = f'Unexpected error: {str(e)}'\n835\t            return ParseResult.make_error(error_msg)\n836\t        finally:\n837\t            # Restore original setting\n838\t            self.enable_scope_tracking = original_setting\n839\t\n840\t    def _track_scopes_from_yaml(self, yaml_content: str):\n841\t        \"\"\"\n842\t        Track scopes by processing YAML content line by line.\n843\t\n844\t        Args:\n845\t            yaml_content: The YAML content to process\n846\t        \"\"\"\n847\t        if not self.scope_stack:\n848\t            return\n849\t\n850\t        lines = yaml_content.split('\\n')\n851\t\n852\t        for line_num, raw_line in enumerate(lines, start=1):\n853\t            trimmed = raw_line.strip()\n854\t\n855\t            # Skip empty lines and comments for key detection\n856\t            is_empty = not trimmed or trimmed.startswith('#')\n857\t\n858\t            # Update current line number for ALL lines (including empty)\n859\t            self.current_line_number = line_num\n860\t\n861\t            # Update current line type only for meaningful lines\n862\t            if not is_empty:\n863\t                line_type = self.scope_stack._classify_line_type(raw_line)\n864\t                self.current_line_type = line_type\n865\t\n866\t            # Get indent level\n867\t            indent = len(raw_line) - len(raw_line.lstrip())\n868\t\n869\t            # Check if line has a key\n870\t            key_context = None\n871\t            if not is_empty:\n872\t                key_context = self.scope_stack._extract_key_context(raw_line)\n873\t\n874\t            has_key = key_context is not None\n875\t\n876\t            # Record indent transition (with line type already captured in IndentTransition)\n877\t            self.scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num, indent, has_key, raw_line)\n878\t\n879\t            # Handle scope transitions based on indent and line type\n880\t            # Use actual_indent for comparison to handle indent-only transitions correctly\n881\t            actual_previous_indent = self.scope_stack.get_actual_indent()\n882\t            scope_current_indent = self.scope_stack.current_indent()\n883\t\n884\t            if indent > scope_current_indent:\n885\t                # Indent increased - handle based on line type\n886\t                if line_type == LineClassification.KEY_BEARING:\n887\t                    # Key-bearing line: indent increase creates a new scope\n888\t                    parent_key = None\n889\t                    if key_context and key_context.get('type') == 'parent_mapping':\n890\t                        parent_key = key_context['key']\n891\t                        # Add parent key to current scope first\n892\t                        try:\n893\t                            self.scope_stack.add_key(parent_key, line_num)\n894\t                        except DuplicateKeyError:\n895\t                            pass  # Ignore duplicate parent keys for now\n896\t\n897\t                    self.scope_stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num, parent_key)\n898\t                    self.scope_depth = self.get_scope_depth()\n899\t                elif line_type == LineClassification.INDENT_ONLY:\n900\t                    # Indent-only line: indent increase does NOT create a new scope\n901\t                    # This is a continuation line - just update tracking, stay in current scope\n902\t                    # The indent increase represents multi-line value content\n903\t                    pass\n904\t                else:\n905\t                    # Empty lines don't trigger scope transitions\n906\t                    pass\n907\t\n908\t            elif indent < actual_previous_indent:\n909\t                # Indent decreased - always exit to parent scope regardless of line type\n910\t                # This ensures proper scope cleanup when returning to outer levels\n911\t                # Use actual_previous_indent to handle indent-only transitions correctly\n912\t                self.scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent)\n913\t                self.scope_depth = self.get_scope_depth()\n914\t\n915\t            else:\n916\t                # Same indent level - add key to current scope if present\n917\t                if key_context and has_key:\n918\t                    key_type = key_context.get('type')\n919\t                    key_name = key_context.get('key')\n920\t\n921\t                    if key_type == 'parent_mapping':\n922\t                        # Parent mapping key creates a new scope for its children\n923\t                        # First add the parent key to the current scope\n924\t                        try:\n925\t                            self.scope_stack.add_key(key_name, line_num)\n926\t during YAML parsing.\n\n    The ScopeStack maintains the hierarchical nature of YAML scopes during parsing.\n    As the parser encounters different indentation levels, it enters and exits scopes,\n    ensuring that duplicate key detection only considers keys within the same scope.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, base_indent: int = 2):\n        \"\"\"\n        Initialize a new scope stack.\n\n return len(self.scopes)\n\n    def current_indent(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Get the current scope-based indent level.\"\"\"\n        if not self.scopes:\n            return 0\n        return self.scopes[-1].indent_level\n\n    def get_actual_indent(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Get the actual indent level of the current line (including indent-only transitions).\"\"\"\n        return empty\")\n        return self.scopes[-1]\n\n    def get_scope_at_level(self, indent_level: int) -> Optional[Scope]:\n        \"\"\"Get scope for a specific indentation level.\"\"\"\n        for scope in self.scopes:\n            if scope.indent_level == indent_level:\n                return scope\n        return None\n\n    def get_scope_path(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get human-readable path to current scope.\"\"\"\n        path_parts = []\n        for scope in self.scopes:\n            if scope.parent_key:\n                path_parts.append(scope.parent_key)\n        return \".\".join(path_parts)\n\n    def enter_scope(self, indent_level: int, line: int, parent_key: Optional[str] = None):\n        \"\"\"\n        Enter a new scope (when indent increases).\n\n        Args:\n            indent_level: The new indentation level\n            line: The line number where this scope starts (1-indexed)\n            parent_key: Optional parent key that created this scope\n        \"\"\"\n        # Check if we already have a scope at this level\n        existing = self.get_scope_at_level(indent_level)\n\n        if existing and parent_key is None:\n            # Re-entering a scope level without parent key - clear and reuse for sibling mappings\n            fresh_scope = Scope(\n                indent_level=indent_level,\n                start_line=line,\n                parent_key=parent_key\n            )\n            fresh_scope.is_flow_style = existing.is_flow_style\n\n            # Remove all scopes deeper than this level\n            self.scopes = [s for s in self.scopes if s.indent_level <= indent_level]\n            self.scopes.append(fresh_scope)\n        elif existing and parent_key is not None:\n            # Parent key exists at this level - don't create duplicate scope\n            # This happens when root-level keys create scopes\n            # Just update the existing scope's parent_key if needed\n            if existing.parent_key is None and parent_key:\n                existing.parent_key = parent_key\n                existing.start_line = line\n        else:\n            # Create new scope\n            new_scope = Scope(\n                indent_level=indent_level,\n                start_line=line,\n                parent_key=parent_key\n            )\n            self.scopes.append(new_scope)\n\n    def enter_sequence_scope(self, indent_level: int, line: int):\n        \"\"\"\n        Enter a sequence context (when we see a `-` item).\n\n        Args:\n            indent_level: The indentation level of the sequence item\n            line: The line number where this sequence item starts (1-indexed)\n        \"\"\"\n        # Remove all scopes at or deeper than this level\n        self.scopes = [s for s in self.scopes if s.indent_level < indent_level]\n\n        # Always create a new scope for each sequence item\n        self.sequence_item_counter += 1\n        new_scope = Scope(\n            indent_level=indent_level,\n            start_line=line,\n            parent_key=None\n        )\n        new_scope.in_sequence_context = True\n        new_scope.sequence_item_id = self.sequence_item_counter\n        self.scopes.append(new_scope)\n\n    def exit_to_scope(self, target_indent: int):\n        \"\"\"\n        Exit to parent scope (when indent decreases).\n\n        Args:\n            target_indent: The indentation level to exit to\n        \"\"\"\n        if target_indent > self.current_indent():\n            # Can't exit to a deeper level\n            return\n\n        # Remove all scopes deeper than target\n        self.scopes = [s for s in self.scopes if s.indent_level <= target_indent]\n\n        # Ensure we have a scope at the target level\n        if not self.scopes:\n            # Create fallback scope if stack would be empty\n            self.scopes.append(Scope(indent_level=0, start_line=0, parent_key=None))\n        elif not any(s.indent_level == target_indent for s in self.scopes):\n            # No exact match - create fallback\n            fallback = Scope(indent_level=target_indent, start_line=0, parent_key=None)\n            self.scopes.append(fallback)\n\n    def exit_one_level(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Exit one level to immediate parent scope.\n\n        Returns:\n            True if successfully exited to parent, False if already at root scope\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.depth() <= 1:\n            return False\n\n        current_indent = self.current_indent()\n\n        # Find the parent scope's indent level\n        if self.depth() >= 2:\n            parent_indent = self.scopes[-2].indent_level\n        else:\n            parent_indent = 0\n\n        self.exit_to_scope(parent_indent)\n        return True\n\n    def scope_exit_to_level(self, target_depth: int) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Exit to a specific scope depth level (grandparent or any ancestor).\n\n        This allows exiting multiple levels at once to reach any valid ancestor scope.\n        For example, from depth 5 you can exit directly to depth 2 (skpping depths 4 and 3).\n\n        Args:\n            target_depth: The target depth level to exit to (1-indexed, where 1 is root scope).\n                          Must be a valid ancestor depth between 1 and current depth.\n\n        Returns:\n            True if successfully exited to target depth\n\n        Raises:\n            ValueError: If target depth is invalid (<= 0, > current depth, or not an ancestor)\n        \"\"\"\n        if target_depth <= 0:\n            raise ValueError(f\"Target depth must be positive (got {target_depth})\")\n\n        current_depth = self.depth()\n\n        if target_depth > current_depth:\n            raise ValueError(\n                f\"Cannot exit to depth {target_depth} (current depth is {current_depth})\"\n            )\n\n        if target_depth == current_depth:\n            # Already at target depth - no action needed\n            return True\n\n        # Convert to 0-indexed and get the target scope\n        target_index = target_depth - 1\n\n        if target_index >= len(self.scopes):\n            raise ValueError(\n                f\"Invalid target depth {target_depth} (no scope at that level)\"\n            )\n\n        # Get the target scope and its indent level\n        target_scope = self.scopes[target_index]\n        target_indent = target_scope.indent_level\n\n        # Exit to that scope's indent level\n        self.exit_to_scope(target_indent)\n\n        return True\n\n    def contains_key(self, key: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if current scope contains a key.\"\"\"\n        if not self.scopes:\n            return False\n        return self.scopes[-1].contains_key(key)\n\n    def contains_key_in_any_scope(self, key: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if any scope in the hierarchy contains a key.\"\"\"\n        return any(scope.contains_key(key) for scope in self.scopes)\n\n    def add_key(self, key: str, line: int) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Add a key to current scope.\n\n        Args:\n            key: The key to add\n            line: The line number where the key appears (1-indexed)\n\n        Raises:\n            DuplicateKeyError: If the key already exists in the current scope\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.contains_key(key):\n            scope = self.current_scope()\n            raise DuplicateKeyError(\n                key=key,\n                scope_path=self.get_scope_path(),\n                first_line=scope.start_line,\n                duplicate_line=line\n            )\n        self.scopes[-1].add_key(key)\n\n    def in_sequence_context(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if we're in a sequence context.\"\"\"\n        if not self.scopes:\n            return False\n        return self.scopes[-1].in_sequence_context\n\n    def record_indent_transition(self, line_number: int, new_indent: int,\n                                 has_key: bool, raw_line: str):\n        \"\"\"\n        Record an indent transition.\n\n        Args:\n            line_number: The line number where this transition occurred (1-indexed)\n            new_indent: The new indentation level\n            has_key: Whether this transition occurred on a line with a key token\n            raw_line: The raw line content (for debugging)\n        \"\"\"\n        # Always update actual_indent to track the current line's indentation\n        # This maintains consistency even for indent-only transitions\n        self.actual_indent = new_indent\n\n        if new_indent != self.last_indent:\n            old_indent = self.last_indent\n            line_classification = self._classify_line_type(raw_line)\n\n            transition = IndentTransition(\n                line_number=line_number,\n                from_indent=old_indent,\n                to_indent=new_indent,\n                has_key=has_key,\n                raw_line=raw_line,\n                line_classification=line_classification,\n                transition_type=self._classify_transition(old_indent, new_indent)\n            )\n            self.indent_transitions.append(transition)\n            self.last_indent = new_indent\n\n    def _classify_transition(self, from_indent: int, to_indent: int) -> IndentTransitionType:\n        \"\"\"Classify an indent transition.\"\"\"\n        if to_indent > from_indent:\n            return IndentTransitionType.ENTER_SCOPE\n        elif to_indent < from_indent:\n            return IndentTransitionType.EXIT_SCOPE\n        else:\n            return IndentTransitionType.SAME_LEVEL\n\n    def _classify_line_type(self, line: str) -> LineClassification:\n        \"\"\"Classify a YAML line as key-bearing or indent-only.\"\"\"\n        trimmed = line.strip()\n\n        if not trimmed:\n            return LineClassification.EMPTY\n\n        # Check if line has a key token\n        if self._extract_key_context(line):\n            return LineClassification.KEY_BEARING\n        else:\n            return LineClassification.INDENT_ONLY\n\n    def _has_key_token(self, line: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Detect if a line contains a key token (colon that starts a key-value pair).\n\n        A key token is a colon that:\n        - Is not inside quotes (single or double)\n        - Is not inside a flow collection ({ } or [ ])\n        - Is not part of a block scalar indicator (| or >)\n        - Is not in a comment (after #)\n        - Has a valid key before it\n\n        Args:\n            line: The line to check\n\n        Returns:\n            True if the line contains a key token, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        trimmed = line.strip()\n\n        # Skip empty lines and comments\n        if not trimmed or trimmed.startswith('#'):\n            return False\n\n        # Check for block scalar indicators at the start (after stripping sequence dash)\n        check_line = trimmed\n        if check_line.startswith('- '):\n            check_line = check_line[2:].strip()\n        elif check_line.startswith('-') and len(check_line) > 1:\n            check_line = check_line[1:].strip()\n\n        if check_line.startswith('|') or check_line.startswith('>'):\n            return False\n\n        # Track state while scanning for colon\n        in_single_quote = False\n        in_double_quote = False\n        flow_depth = 0  # Depth of nested {} or []\n\n        for i, char in enumerate(trimmed):\n            # Handle quote state changes\n            if char == '\"' and not in_single_quote:\n                # Toggle double quote (unless escaped)\n                if i == 0 or trimmed[i - 1] != '\\\\':\n                    in_double_quote = not in_double_quote\n            elif char == \"'\" and not in_double_quote:\n                in_single_quote = not in_single_quote\n\n            # Track flow collection depth (only outside quotes)\n            if not in_single_quote and not in_double_quote:\n                if char == '#':\n                    # Rest of line is a comment - stop looking\n                    break\n                elif char in '{[':\n                    flow_depth += 1\n                elif char in '}]':\n                    flow_depth = max(0, flow_depth - 1)\n\n            # Check for colon as key token\n            if char == ':' and not in_single_quote and not in_double_quote and flow_depth == 0:\n                # Check if this could be a key token\n                key_part = trimmed[:i].strip()\n\n                # Validate the key\n                if self._is_valid_key(key_part):\n                    # Check what comes after the colon\n                    if i + 1 >= len(trimmed):\n                        # Colon at end of line - this is a key token (parent mapping)\n                        return True\n                    else:\n                        next_char = trimmed[i + 1]\n                        # Colon followed by whitespace or end of line\n                        if next_char.isspace() or next_char == '#':\n                            return True\n\n        return False\n\n    def _is_valid_key(self, key_part: str) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if a key part is a valid YAML key.\n\n        Args:\n            key_part: The text before a colon\n\n        Returns:\n            True if this is a valid key, False otherwise\n        \"\"\"\n        if not key_part:\n            return False\n\n        key = key_part\n\n        # Remove sequence dash prefix if present\n        if key.startswith(\"- \"):\n            key = key[2:].strip()\n        elif key.startswith('-'):\n            # Handle both single dash and dash followed by non-space\n            key = key[1:].strip()\n\n        # Key must not be empty after stripping dash\n        if not key:\n            return False\n\n        # Key must not contain flow collection markers\n        if any(c in key for c in ['{', '}', '[', ']']):\n            return False\n\n        # Key must not contain comment or block scalar markers\n        if any(c in key for c in ['#', '|', '>']):\n            return False\n\n        # Key must not be just special characters\n        if not any(c.isalnum() or c in '_-.' for c in key):\n            return False\n\n        return True\n\n    def _extract_key_context(self, line: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Extract key context from a line.\n\n        Returns:\n            Dict with key context info, or None if no key found\n        \"\"\"\n        trimmed = line.strip()\n\n        # Find colon position using improved key token detection\n        if not self._has_key_token(line):\n            return None\n\n        # Find the actual colon position\n        colon_pos = trimmed.find(':')\n        if colon_pos == -1:\n            return None\n\n        key_part = trimmed[:colon_pos]\n        after_colon = trimmed[colon_pos + 1:]\n\n        # Strip sequence dash from key if present\n        key = key_part.strip()\n        if key.startswith(\"- \"):\n            key = key[2:].strip()\n        elif key.startswith('-') and len(key) > 1:\n            key = key[1:].strip()\n\n        if not key:\n            return None\n\n        # Classify based on what comes after the colon\n        if after_colon.strip():\n            return {\n                'type': 'inline_scalar',\n                'key': key,\n                'value': after_colon.strip()\n            }\n        else:\n            return {\n                'type': 'parent_mapping',\n                'key': key\n            }\n\n    def get_indent_transitions(self) -> List[IndentTransition]:\n        \"\"\"Get all recorded indent transitions.\"\"\"\n        return self.indent_transitions.copy()\n\n    def clear_indent_transitions(self):\n        \"\"\"Clear all indent transitions.\"\"\"\n        self.indent_transitions.clear()\n        self.last_indent = 0\n\n    def reset(self):\n        \"\"\"Clear all scopes and reset to root.\"\"\"\n        self.scopes = [Scope(indent_level=0, start_line=0, parent_key=None)]\n        self.clear_indent_transitions()\n\n\nclass YAMLParser:\n    \"\"\"\n    YAML parser with safe_load wrapper, error handling, and scope depth tracking.\n\n    Uses yaml.safe_load() to safely parse YAML files without\n    executing arbitrary Python objects, and provides scope tracking\n    for hierarchical key management.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, enable_scope_tracking: bool = False, base_indent: int = 2):\n        \"\"\"\n        Initialize the YAML parser.\n\n        Args:\n            enable_scope_tracking: Whether to enable scope depth tracking during parsing\n            base_indent: The base indentation size for scope tracking (usually 2 or 4)\n        \"\"\"\n        self.yaml = None\n        self._import_yaml()\n\n        # Scope tracking state\n        self.enable_scope_tracking = enable_scope_tracking\n        self.scope_stack: Optional[ScopeStack] = None\n        self.base_indent = base_indent\n        self.scope_depth: int = 0\n        self.current_line_type: Optional[LineClassification] = None\n        self.current_line_number: int = 0\n\n    def _import_yaml(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Import PyYAML module with fallback handling.\n\n        Raises:\n            RuntimeError: If PyYAML is not available\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            import yaml\n            self.yaml = yaml\n        except ImportError:\n            raise RuntimeError(\n                \"PyYAML is required but not available. \"\n                \"Install it via: pip install pyyaml\"\n            )\n\n    def parse_string(self, yaml_content: str) -> ParseResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Parse YAML from a string.\n\n        Args:\n            yaml_content: String containing YAML content\n\n        Returns:\n            ParseResult with status, data, and error fields\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self.yaml:\n            return ParseResult.make_error('PyYAML not available')\n\n        if not yaml_content or not yaml_content.strip():\n            return ParseResult.make_error('Empty YAML content')\n\n        try:\n            data = self.yaml.safe_load(yaml_content)\n            return ParseResult.success(data)\n        except self.yaml.YAMLError as e:\n            error_msg = self._format_yaml_error(str(e))\n            return ParseResult.make_error(error_msg)\n        except Exception as e:\n            return ParseResult.make_error(f'Unexpected error: {str(e)}')\n\n    def parse_file(self, filepath: str) -> ParseResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Parse YAML from a file.\n\n        Args:\n            filepath: Path to the YAML file\n\n        Returns:\n            ParseResult with status, data, and error fields\n        \"\"\"\n        path = Path(filepath)\n\n        # Check if file exists\n        if not path.exists():\n            return ParseResult.make_error(f'File not found: {filepath}')\n\n        # Check if it's a file (not directory)\n        if not path.is_file():\n            return ParseResult.make_error(f'Path is not a file: {filepath}')\n\n        try:\n            with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:\n                content = f.read()\n\n            return self.parse_string(content)\n\n        except FileNotFoundError:\n            return ParseResult.make_error(f'File not found: {filepath}')\n        except PermissionError:\n            return ParseResult.make_error(f'Permission denied: {filepath}')\n        except UnicodeDecodeError as e:\n            return ParseResult.make_error(f'Encoding error reading file: {str(e)}')\n        except Exception as e:\n            return ParseResult.make_error(f'Error reading file: {str(e)}')\n\n    def _format_yaml_error(self, error_message: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Format YAML error message for better readability.\n\n        Args:\n            error_message: Raw error message from PyYAML\n\n        Returns:\n            Formatted error message\n        \"\"\"\n        # Clean up common PyYAML error patterns\n        error_message = error_message.strip()\n\n        # Add context for common errors\n        if 'could not find expected' in error_message.lower():\n            return f\"YAML syntax error: {error_message}. Check indentation and structure.\"\n        elif 'mapping values are not allowed here' in error_message.lower():\n            return f\"YAML structure error: {error_message}. Check colons and indentation.\"\n        elif 'duplicate key' in error_message.lower():\n            return f\"YAML validation error: {error_message}\"\n\n        return f\"YAML parsing error: {error_message}\"\n\n    # Scope tracking methods\n\n    def _init_scope_tracking(self):\n        \"\"\"Initialize scope tracking for a new parse operation.\"\"\"\n        if self.enable_scope_tracking:\n            self.scope_stack = ScopeStack(base_indent=self.base_indent)\n            self.scope_depth = 0\n            self.current_line_type = None\n            self.current_line_number = 0\n        else:\n            self.scope_stack = None\n            self.scope_depth = 0\n            self.current_line_type = None\n            self.current_line_number = 0\n\n    def get_scope_depth(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get the current scope depth during parsing.\n\n        Returns:\n            The current nesting depth (includes root scope as depth 1)\n            Returns 0 if scope tracking is not enabled.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.scope_stack:\n            return self.scope_stack.depth()  # Include all scopes including root\n        return 0\n\n    def get_scope_path(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get the current scope path as a dot-separated string.\n\n        Returns:\n            A string like \"services.web.database\" representing the scope hierarchy.\n            Returns empty string if scope tracking is not enabled.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.scope_stack:\n            return self.scope_stack.get_scope_path()\n        return \"\"\n\n    def get_parent_scope(self) -> Optional[str]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get the parent scope reference.\n\n        Returns:\n            The parent scope key if available, None otherwise.\n        \"\"\"\n        if self.scope_stack and self.scope_stack.depth() > 1:\n            # Get the second-to-last scope's parent_key (current scope's parent)\n            return self.scope_stack.scopes[-2].parent_key if len(self.scope_stack.scopes) > 1 else None\n        return None\n\n    def get_scope_stack(self) -> Optional[ScopeStack]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get the current scope stack.\n\n        Returns:\n            The ScopeStack instance if scope tracking is enabled, None otherwise.\n        \"\"\"\n        return self.scope_stack\n\n    def parse_with_scope_tracking(self, yaml_content: str) -> ParseResult:\n        \"\"\"\n        Parse YAML content with scope depth tracking enabled.\n\n        This method parses the YAML content line-by-line to track scope depth\n        and hierarchy during parsing. It returns both the parsed data and\n        scope tracking information.\n\n        Args:\n            yaml_content: String containing YAML content\n\n        Returns:\n            ParseResult with status, data, and error fields. If successful,\n            the parser's scope tracking state will be populated.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self.yaml:\n            return ParseResult.make_error('PyYAML not available')\n\n        if not yaml_content or not yaml_content.strip():\n            return ParseResult.make_error('Empty YAML content')\n\n        # Initialize scope tracking\n        original_setting = self.enable_scope_tracking\n        self.enable_scope_tracking = True\n        self._init_scope_tracking()\n\n        try:\n            # First, do the regular parse\n            data = self.yaml.safe_load(yaml_content)\n\n            # Then, track scopes by processing line by line\n            self._track_scopes_from_yaml(yaml_content)\n\n            return ParseResult.success(data)\n        except self.yaml.YAMLError as e:\n            error_msg = self._format_yaml_error(str(e))\n            return ParseResult.make_error(error_msg)\n        except Exception as e:\n            error_msg = f'Unexpected error: {str(e)}'\n            return ParseResult.make_error(error_msg)\n        finally:\n            # Restore original setting\n            self.enable_scope_tracking = original_setting\n\n    def _track_scopes_from_yaml(self, yaml_content: str):\n        \"\"\"\n        Track scopes by processing YAML content line by line.\n\n        Args:\n            yaml_content: The YAML content to process\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self.scope_stack:\n            return\n\n        lines = yaml_content.split('\\n')\n\n        for line_num, raw_line in enumerate(lines, start=1):\n            trimmed = raw_line.strip()\n\n            # Skip empty lines and comments for key detection\n            is_empty = not trimmed or trimmed.startswith('#')\n\n            # Update current line number for ALL lines (including empty)\n            self.current_line_number = line_num\n\n            # Update current line type only for meaningful lines\n            if not is_empty:\n                line_type = self.scope_stack._classify_line_type(raw_line)\n                self.current_line_type = line_type\n\n            # Get indent level\n            indent = len(raw_line) - len(raw_line.lstrip())\n\n            # Check if line has a key\n            key_context = None\n            if not is_empty:\n                key_context = self.scope_stack._extract_key_context(raw_line)\n\n            has_key = key_context is not None\n\n            # Record indent transition (with line type already captured in IndentTransition)\n            self.scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num, indent, has_key, raw_line)\n\n            # Handle scope transitions based on indent and line type\n            # Use actual_indent for comparison to handle indent-only transitions correctly\n            actual_previous_indent = self.scope_stack.get_actual_indent()\n            scope_current_indent = self.scope_stack.current_indent()\n\n            if indent > scope_current_indent:\n                # Indent increased - handle based on line type\n                if line_type == LineClassification.KEY_BEARING:\n                    # Key-bearing line: indent increase creates a new scope\n                    parent_key = None\n                    if key_context and key_context.get('type') == 'parent_mapping':\n                        parent_key = key_context['key']\n                        # Add parent key to current scope first\n                        try:\n                            self.scope_stack.add_key(parent_key, line_num)\n                        except DuplicateKeyError:\n                            pass  # Ignore duplicate parent keys for now\n\n                    self.scope_stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num, parent_key)\n                    self.scope_depth = self.get_scope_depth()\n                elif line_type == LineClassification.INDENT_ONLY:\n                    # Indent-only line: indent increase does NOT create a new scope\n                    # This is a continuation line - just update tracking, stay in current scope\n                    # The indent increase represents multi-line value content\n                    pass\n                else:\n                    # Empty lines don't trigger scope transitions\n                    pass\n\n            elif indent < actual_previous_indent:\n                # Indent decreased - always exit to parent scope regardless of line type\n                # This ensures proper scope cleanup when returning to outer levels\n                # Use actual_previous_indent to handle indent-only transitions correctly\n                self.scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent)\n                self.scope_depth = self.get_scope_depth()\n\n            else:\n                # Same indent level - add key to current scope if present\n                if key_context and has_key:\n                    key_type = key_context.get('type')\n                    key_name = key_context.get('key')\n\n                    if key_type == 'parent_mapping':\n                        # Parent mapping key creates a new scope for its children\n                        # First add the parent key to the current scope\n                        try:\n                            self.scope_stack.add_key(key_name, line_num)\n                        except DuplicateKeyError:\n                            pass  # Ignore duplicate keys for now\n\n                        # Create a new scope for this parent mapping\n                        # This scope will hold the parent's children\n                        self.scope_stack.enter_scope(indent, line_num, key_name)\n                        self.scope_depth = self.get_scope_depth()\n\n                    elif key_type == 'inline_scalar':\n                        # Inline scalar key - just add to current scope\n                        try:\n                            self.scope_stack.add_key(key_name, line_num)\n                        except DuplicateKeyError:\n                            pass  # Ignore for now\n\n    def get_scope_summary(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get a summary of the current scope state.\n\n        Returns:\n            A dictionary containing scope tracking information:\n            - depth: Current scope depth\n            - path: Current scope path\n            - stack_size: Number of scopes in the stack\n            - in_sequence: Whether currently in a sequence context\n            - current_line_type: Line type of the most recently processed line\n            - current_line_number: Line number of the most recently processed line\n            Returns empty dict if scope tracking is not enabled.\n        \"\"\"\n        if not self.scope_stack:\n            return {}\n\n        return {\n            'depth': self.get_scope_depth(),\n            'path': self.get_scope_path(),\n            'stack_size': self.scope_stack.depth(),\n            'in_sequence': self.scope_stack.in_sequence_context(),\n            'current_line_type': self.current_line_type.value if self.current_line_type else None,\n            'current_line_number': self.current_line_number,\n        }\n\n    def get_current_line_type(self) -> Optional[LineClassification]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get the line type of the most recently processed line.\n\n        Returns:\n            The LineClassification of the current line, or None if no line has been processed\n            or scope tracking is not enabled.\n        \"\"\"\n        return self.current_line_type\n\n    def get_current_line_number(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get the line number of the most recently processed line.\n\n        Returns:\n            The 1-indexed line number of the current line, or 0 if no line has been processed.\n        \"\"\"\n        return self.current_line_number\n\n    def is_on_key_bearing_line(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if the parser is currently on a key-bearing line.\n\n        Returns:\n            True if the current line is key-bearing, False otherwise.\n            Returns False if scope tracking is not enabled or no line has been processed.\n        \"\"\"\n        return self.current_line_type == LineClassification.KEY_BEARING\n\n    def is_on_indent_only_line(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if the parser is currently on an indent-only line.\n\n        Returns:\n            True if the current line is indent-only, False otherwise.\n            Returns False if scope tracking is not enabled or no line has been processed.\n        \"\"\"\n        return self.current_line_type == LineClassification.INDENT_ONLY\n\n    def is_on_empty_line(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Check if the parser is currently on an empty line.\n\n        Returns:\n            True if the current line is empty, False otherwise.\n            Returns False if scope tracking is not enabled or no line has been processed.\n        \"\"\"\n        return self.current_line_type == LineClassification.EMPTY\n","numLines":1015,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1015}}}

and directives\n268\t            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" || trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n269\t                // Update state to track that we're on an empty line (marker
