line_type;\n279\t\n280\t            // Only track actual indent changes\n281\t            if indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent() {\n282\t                let has_key = line_type.is_key_bearing();\n283\t                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n284\t\n285\t                // Update transition state to track this indent change\n286\t                let from_indent = scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n287\t                self.current_transition_state.update(from_indent, indent, has_key);\n288\t            }\n289\t        }\n290\t\n291\t        scope_stack.get_indent_transitions().to_vec()\n292\t    }\n293\t\n294\t    /// Get transition counts by type\n295\t    ///\n296\t    /// Returns (enter_scope_count, exit_scope_count, same_level_count)\n297\t    /// Updates parser state with line classifications during counting.\n298\t    pub fn get_transition_counts(&mut self, content: &str) -> (usize, usize, usize) {\n299\t        let transitions = self.get_indent_transitions(content);\n300\t\n301\t        let enter = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_enter_scope()).count();\n302\t        let exit = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_exit_scope()).count();\n303\t        let same = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_same_level()).count();\n304\t\n305\t        (enter, exit, same)\n306\t    }\n307\t\n308\t    /// Detect duplicate keys using scope-aware tracking\n309\t    ///\n310\t    /// This method processes YAML content line-by-line, tracking keys within\n311\t    /// their proper scope contexts. It returns a list of validation errors\n312\t    /// for any duplicate keys found within the same scope.\n313\t    fn detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(&self, content: &str, scope_stack: &mut ScopeStack) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n314\t        let mut duplicate_errors = Vec::new();\n315\t\n316\t        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n317\t            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n318\t            let trimmed = line.trim();\n319\t\n320\t            // Classify the line type to enable type-specific scope handling\n321\t            let line_type = classify_line_type(line);\n322\t\n323\t            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n324\t            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n325\t                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n326\t                {\n327\t                    log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n328\t                }\n329\t                scope_stack.reset();\n330\t                continue;\n331\t            }\n332\t\n333\t            // Skip YAML directives\n334\t            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n335\t                continue;\n336\t            }\n337\t\n338\t            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n339\t\n340\t            // Track ALL indent changes for detection purposes, regardless of line type\n341\t            // This enables analysis of indent transitions even on blank lines, comments, etc.\n342\t            let indent_changed = indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n343\t            if indent_changed {\n344\t                // Use line classification to determine if this line has a key\n345\t                let has_key = line_type.is_key_bearing();\n346\t                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n347\t            }\n348\t\n349\t            // Handle blank lines and comments with type-specific logic\n350\t            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n351\t            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n352\t            // Comments are also transparent to scope tracking since YAML parsers ignore them.\n353\t            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n354\t                // Update last_indent tracking so the next line can detect indent changes\n355\t                // but don't trigger any scope transitions\n356\t                if indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent() {\n357\t                    scope_stack.set_last_indent(indent);\n358\t                }\n359\t                continue;\n360\t            }\n361\t\n362\t            // Type-specific handling: indent-only lines (no key token)\n363\t            // These lines don't trigger scope entry but may trigger scope exit on indent decrease\n364\t            if !line_type.is_key_bearing() {\n365\t                // Indent-only line - handle scope exit if indent decreased\n366\t                if indent < scope_stack.current_indent() {\n367\t                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n368\t                }\n369\t                continue;\n370\t            }\n371\t\n372\t            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n373\t            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n374\t            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n375\t                Ordering::Greater => {\n376\t                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n377\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n378\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n379\t                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n380\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n381\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n382\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n383\t                                    dup_err.message()\n384\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n385\t                            }\n386\t                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n387\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n388\t                            {\n389\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n390\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n391\t                            }\n392\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n393\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n394\t                                line_num_1index,\n395\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n396\t                            );\n397\t                        } else {\n398\t                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n399\t                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n400\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n401\t                            {\n402\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n403\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n404\t                            }\n405\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n406\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n407\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n408\t                                    dup_err.message()\n409\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n410\t                            }\n411\t                        }\n412\t                    } else {\n413\t                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n414\t                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n415\t                        // - A scalar value continuation\n416\t                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n417\t                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n418\t                        {\n419\t                            log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n420\t                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n421\t                        }\n422\t                    }\n423\t                }\n424\t                Ordering::Less => {\n425\t                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n426\t                    // Check if we're transitioning to a sequence item at same level as parent\n427\t                    // In this case, we should NOT exit the parent scope\n428\t                    let trimmed = line.trim();\n429\t                    let is_sequence_item_at_parent_level = trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") &&\n430\t                        scope_stack.current_indent() == indent + scope_stack.base_indent();\n431\t\n432\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n433\t                    {\n434\t                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n435\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}', is_sequence_item_at_parent_level={}\",\n436\t                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path, is_sequence_item_at_parent_level);\n437\t                    }\n438\t\n439\t                    if !is_sequence_item_at_parent_level {\n440\t                        scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n441\t                    }\n442\t\n443\t                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n444\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n445\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n446\t                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n447\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n448\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n449\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n450\t                                    dup_err.message()\n451\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n452\t                            }\n453\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n454\t                            {\n455\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n456\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n457\t                            }\n458\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n459\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n460\t                                line_num_1index,\n461\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n462\t                            );\n463\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n464\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n465\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n466\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n467\t                                    dup_err.message()\n468\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n469\t                            }\n470\t                        }\n471\t                    }\n472\t                    // Edge case: indent decreased but no key - we've already exited to the right scope\n473\t                }\n474\t                Ordering::Equal => {\n475\t                    // Same scope - check for keys\n476\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n477\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n478\t                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n479\t                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n480\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n481\t                            {\n482\t                                let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n483\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit for sibling: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n484\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n485\t                            }\n486\t                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n487\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n488\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n489\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n490\t                                    dup_err.message()\n491\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n492\t                            }\n493\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n494\t                            {\n495\t                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n496\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n497\t                            }\n498\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n499\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n500\t                                line_num_1index,\n501\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n502\t                            );\n503\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n504\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n505\t                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n506\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n507\t                                    dup_err.message()\n508\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n509\t                            }\n510\t                        }\n511\t                    }\n512\t                    // Edge case: same indent but no key - just continue, no scope change needed\n513\t                }\n514\t            }\n515\t\n516\t            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n517\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n518\t                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n519\t                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n520\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n521\t                    {\n522\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n523\t                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n524\t                    }\n525\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n526\t                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n527\t                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n528\t                        duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n529\t                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n530\t                            dup_err.message()\n531\t                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n532\t                    }\n533\t                } else {\n534\t                    // Sequence item without a key context\n535\t                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n536\t                    {\n537\t                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n538\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n539\t                    }\n540\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n541\t                }\n542\t            }\n543\t        }\n544\t\n545\t        duplicate_errors\n546\t    }\n547\t}\n548\t\n549\timpl Default for BasicParser {\n550\t    fn default() -> Self {\n551\t        Self::new()\n552\t    }\n553\t}\n554\t\n555\timpl Parser for BasicParser {\n556\t    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n557\t        // Create a new scope stack for this parsing               scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n614\t                }\n615\t                continue;\n616\t            }\n617\t\n618\t            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation increased - entering deeper scope\n623\t                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n624\t                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n625\t                            //                   ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n631\t                            }\n632\t                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n633\t             }\n711\t                    }\n712\t                }\n713\t                Ordering::Equal => {\n714\t                    // Same scope - check for keys\n715\t                    if let                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n727\t                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n728\t                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n729\t                                    dup_err.message()\n730\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n731\t                            }\n732\t                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n733\t                            {\n734\t                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n735\t                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n736\t                            }\n737\t                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n738\t                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n739\t                                line_num_1index,\n740\t                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n741\t                            );\n742\t                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n743\t                            if let Err(dup_err) = line_num_1index),\n746\t                                    dup_err.message()\n747\t                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n748\t                            }\n749\t                        }\n750\t                    }\n751\t                }\n752\t            }\n753\t\n754\t            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n755\t            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n756\t                // When starting a new sequence item, exit any existing sequence scope at the same indent\n757\t                if scope_stack.current_indent() == #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n784\t                    {\n785\t                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n786\t                            indent, line_num_1index);\n787\t                    }\n788\t                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n789\t                }\n790\t            }\n791\t        }\n792\t\n793\t        // Return parse result using serde_yaml for the actual value\n794\t        match serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(content) {\n795\t            Ok(value) => {\n796\t                // Note: parse_errors from scope tracking are currently informational\n797\t                // They could be added as warnings in a future enhancement\n798\t                ParseResult::success(value)\n799\t            }\n800\t            Err(err) => {\n801\t                // Add any duplicate key errors we found to the parse error\n802\t                let error_msg = if parse_errors.is_empty() {\n803\t                    err.to_string()\n804\t                } else {\n805\t                    format!(\"{}; duplicate key errors: {}\", err, parse_errors.iter()\n806\t                        .map(|e| e.message.clone()).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(\"; \"))\n807\t                };\n808\t                ParseResult::failure(ParseError::syntax(error_msg))\n809\t            }\n810\t        }\n811\t    }\n812\t\n813\t    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n814\t        // Convert bytes to string and parse\n815\t        match std::str::from_utf8(content) {\n816\t            Ok(utf8_content) => self.parse_str(utf8_content),\n817\t            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Invalid UTF-8: {}\", err))),\n818\t        }\n819\t    }\n820\t\n821\t    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n822\t        // Read file content\n823\t        match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n824\t            Ok(content) => self.parse_str(&content),\n825\t            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Failed to read file {}: {}\", path.display(), err))),\n826\t        }\n827\t    }\n828\t\n829\t    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n830\t        // Create syntax validator based on parser mode\n831\t        let validator = if self.config.is_strict() {\n832\t            SyntaxValidator::strict()\n833\t        } else {\n834\t            SyntaxValidator::lenient()\n835\t        };\n836\t\n837\t        // Run syntax validation\n838\t        let mut result = validator.validate(content);\n839\t\n840\t        // Run scope-aware duplicate key detection (when duplicates are not allowed)\n841\t        if !self.config.allow_duplicates {\n842\t            let mut scope_stack = self.scope_stack.clone();\n843\t            let duplicate_errors = self.detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(content, &mut scope_stack);\n844\t            if !duplicate_errors.is_empty() {\n845\t                result.valid = false;\n846\t                result.errors.extend(duplicate_errors);\n847\t            }\n848\t        }\n849\t\n850\t        // If no errors from basic validation, run enhanced detection\n851\t        if result.is_valid() {\n852\t            let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n853\t            let detector_result = detector.detect_to_validation_result(content);\n854\t\n855\t            // Merge errors from detector\n856\t            if !detector_result.is_valid() {\n857\t                result.valid = false;\n858\t                result.errors.extend(detector_result.errors);\n859\t            }\n860\t        }\n861\t\n862\t        result\n863\t    }\n864\t\n865\t    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult {\n866\t        // Read file content\n867\t        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n868\t            Ok(content) => content,\n869\t            Err(err) => {\n870\t                return ValidationResult {\n871\t                    valid: false,\n872\t                    errors: vec![ValidationError::new(\n873\t                        path.display().to_string(),\n874\t                        format!(\"failed to read file: {}\", err)\n875\t                    )],\n876\t                    warnings: Vec::new(),\n877\t                };\n878\t            }\n879\t        };\n880\t\n881\t        // Validate the content\n882\t        self.validate_str(&content)\n883\t    }\n884\t\n885\t    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig {\n886\t        &self.config\n887\t    }\n888\t\n889\t    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n890\t    where\n891\t        Self: Sized,\n892\t    {\n893\t        Self {\n894\t            config,\n895\t            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n896\t            current_line_type: LineClassification::Empty,\n897\t            current_transition_state: IndentTransitionState::new(),\n898\t            scope_depth: 1, // Root scope is always present\n899\t        }\n900\t    }\n901\t}\n902\t\n903\t// ============================================================================\n904\t// Integration Tests for Scope Tracking\n905\t// ============================================================================\n906\t\n907\t#[cfg(test)]\n908\tmod integration_tests {\n909\t    use super::*;\n910\t\n911\t    /// Test parse_str with r#\"\n1666\troot:\n1667\t  level1:\n1668\t    level2: value\n1669\t  level1_sibling: value2\n1670\t\"#;\n1671\t\n1672\t        let transitions = parser.get_indent_transitions(yaml);\n1673\t\n1674\t        // Should have transitions for indent changes\n1675\t        assert!(!transitions.is_empty(), \"Should track indent transitions\");\n1676\t\n1677\t        // Verify transitions are properly BasicParser::new();\n1695\t\n1696\t        let yaml = r#\"\n1697\tkey1: value1\n1698\t  nested:\n1699\t    deep: value\n1700\tkey2: value2\n1701\t\"#;\n1702\t\n1703\t        let transitions = parser.get_indent_transitions(yaml);\n1704\t\n1705\t        // Check that all transitions are properly classified\n1706\t        for transition in &transitions {\n1707\t            let transition_type = transition.transition_type();\n1708\t\n1709\t            // Verify classification matches indent change\n1710\t            if transition.to_indent > transition.from_indent {\n1711\t                assert_eq!(transition_type, IndentTransitionType::EnterScope,\n1712\t                           \"Indent increase should be classified as EnterScope\");\n1713\t            } else if transition.to_indent < transition.from_indent {\n1714\t                assert_eq!(transition_type, IndentTransitionType::ExitScope,\n1715\t                           \"Indent decrease should be classified as ExitScope\");\n1716\t            } else {\n1717\t                assert_eq!(transition_type, IndentTransitionType::SameLevel,\n1718\t                           \"No indent change should be classified as SameLevel\");\n1719\t            }\n1720\t        }\n1721\t    }\n1722\t\n1723\t    /// Test scope operation mapping\n1724\t    #[test]\n1725\t    fn test_scope_operation_mapping() {\n1726\t        let mut parser = BasicParser::new();\n1727\t\n1728\t        let yaml = r#\"\n1729\tparent:\n1730\t  child: value\n1731\tsibling: value2\n1732\t\"#;\n1733\t\n1734\t        let transitions = parser.get_indent_transitions(yaml);\n1735\t\n1736\t        // Verify each transition has a valid scope operation\n1737\t        for transition in &transitions {\n1738\t            let operation = transition.scope_operation();\n1739\t\n1740\t            match operation {\n1741\t                \"enter-scope\" => assert!(transition.is_enter_scope()),\n1742\t                \"exit-scope\" => assert!(transition.is_exit_scope()),\n1743\t                \"stay-in-scope\" => assert!(transition.is_same_level()),\n1744\t                _ => panic!(\"Invalid scope operation: {}\", operation),\n1745\t            }\n1746\t        }\n1747\t    }\n1748\t\n1749\t    /// Test transition counts by type\n1750\t    #[test]\n1751\t    fn test_transition_counts() {\n1752\t value\n1761\t\"#;\n1762\t\n1763\t        let (enter, exit, same) = parser.get_transition_counts(yaml);\n1764\t\n1765\t        // Should have multiple transitions\n1766\t        assert!(enter > 0, \"Should have enter-scope transitions\");\n1767\t        assert!(exit > 0, \"Should have exit-scope transitions\");\n1768\t\n1769\t        // Enter and exit should be roughly balanced (with small /// Test transitions with blank lines\n1775\t    #[test]\n1776\t    fn test_transitions_with_blank_lines() {\n1777\t        let mut parser = BasicParser::new();\n1778\t\n1779\t        let yaml = history is maintained\n1925\t    #[test]\n1926\t    fn test_transition_history_maintained() {\n1927\t        let mut parser = BasicParser::new();\n1928\t\n1929\t        let yaml = r#\"\n1930\ta:\n1931\t  b:\n1932\t    c: value\n1933\t  d: value\n1934\te: value\n1935\t\"#;\n1936\t\n1937\t        let transitions = parser.get_indent_transitions(yaml);\n1938\t\n1939\t        // Transitions should be in order\n1940\t        let mut last_line = 0;\n1941\t        for (i, transition) in transitions.iter().enumerate() {\n1942\t            assert!(transition.line_number > last_line,\n1943\t                   \"Transition {} should be after previous transition\", i);\n1944\t            last_line = transition.line_number;\n1945\t        }\n1946\t\n1947\t        // Each transition should have complete information\n1948\t        for transition in &transitions {\n1949\t            assert!(transition.line_number > 0);\n1950\t            assert!(transition.from_indent <= transition.to_indent ||\n1951\t                   transition.to_indent <= transition.from_indent);\n1952\t            assert!(!transition.raw_line.is_empty());\n1953\t        }\n1954\t  fn test_scope_depth_with_nested_structures() {\n2040\t        let mut parser = BasicParser::new();\n2041\t\n2042\t        let yaml = r#\"\n2043\ta:\n2044\t  b:\n2045\t    c:\n2046\t      d: }\n2127\t\n2128\t    /// Test parent scope at different offsets\n2129\t    #[test]\n2130\t    fn test_parent_scope_at_different_offsets() {\n2131\t        let parser = assert!(parser.parent_scope(1).is_none(), \"Offset 1 should return None at root\");\n2136\t        assert!(parser.parent_scope(10).is_none(), \"Large offset should return None\");\n2137\t Core parser trait and implementations for YAML parsing\n//!\n//! This module defines the Parser trait and provides functionality for\n//! parsing YAML content from various sources.\n\nuse crate::parsers::yaml::{\n    types::{ParseResult, ValidationResult, ValidationError},\n    error::ParseError,\n    ParserConfig,\n    syntax_validator::SyntaxValidator,\n    syntax_detector::SyntaxDetector,\n    scope::{ScopeStack, classify_line_type, LineClassification, IndentTransitionState, IndentTransitionType},\n    line_parser::{calculate_indentation},\n    scope::extract_key_context,\n};\n\n#[cfg(debug_assertions)]\nuse log::debug as log_debug;\n\n/// Trait for YAML parsers\n///\n/// This trait defines the core interface for parsing YAML content.\n/// Implementations can parse from strings, files, or other sources.\npub trait Parser {\n    /// Parse YAML content from a string\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `content` - The YAML content as a string\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A ParseResult containing the parsed data or an error\n    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Parse YAML content from a byte slice\n    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Parse YAML content from a file\n    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value>;\n\n    /// Validate YAML content without fully parsing it\n    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult;\n\n    /// Validate a YAML file without fully parsing it\n    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult;\n\n    /// Get the parser configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// A reference to the parser's configuration\n    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig;\n\n    /// Set the parser configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    /// * `config` - The new configuration\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    /// The parser with the new configuration\n    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n    where\n        Self: Sized;\n}\n\n/// Basic YAML parser implementation\n///\n/// This is a minimal implementation of the Parser trait that\n/// provides basic YAML parsing functionality with scope-aware key tracking.\n#[derive(Debug, Clone)]\npub struct BasicParser {\n    config: ParserConfig,\n    /// Scope stack for tracking keys within their proper scope contexts\n    scope_stack: ScopeStack,\n    /// Current line type being processed (key-bearing vs indent-only)\n    current_line_type: LineClassification,\n    /// Current indent transition state for tracking scope operations\n    current_transition_state: IndentTransitionState,\n    /// Current scope depth (number of active scopes in the hierarchy)\n    scope_depth: usize,\n}\n\nimpl BasicParser {\n    /// Create a new BasicParser with default configuration\n    pub fn new() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: ParserConfig::default(),\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n            current_line_type: LineClassification::Empty,\n            current_transition_state: IndentTransitionState::new(),\n            scope_depth: 1, // Root scope is always present\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new BasicParser with the specified configuration\n    pub fn with_config(config: ParserConfig) -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n            current_line_type: LineClassification::Empty,\n            current_transition_state: IndentTransitionState::new(),\n            scope_depth: 1, // Root scope is always present\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Create a new strict parser\n    ///\n    /// A strict parser enables strict mode and disallows duplicate keys.\n    /// Uses the comprehensive strict configuration from ParserConfig.\n    pub fn strict() -> Self {\n        Self {\n            config: ParserConfig::strict(),\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n            current_line_type: LineClassification::Empty,\n            current_transition_state: IndentTransitionState::new(),\n            scope_depth: 1, // Root scope is always present\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current line type being processed\n    pub fn current_line_type(&self) -> LineClassification {\n        self.current_line_type\n    }\n\n    /// Check if current line is key-bearing\n    pub fn is_key_bearing_line(&self) -> bool {\n        self.current_line_type.is_key_bearing()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if current line is indent-only\n    pub fn is_indent_only_line(&self) -> bool {\n        self.current_line_type.is_indent_only()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if current line is empty\n    pub fn is_empty_line(&self) -> bool {\n        self.current_line_type.is_empty()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current indent transition state\n    pub fn current_transition_state(&self) -> &IndentTransitionState {\n        &self.current_transition_state\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the parser is currently entering a scope\n    pub fn is_entering_scope(&self) -> bool {\n        self.current_transition_state.is_entering_scope()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the parser is currently exiting a scope\n    pub fn is_exiting_scope(&self) -> bool {\n        self.current_transition_state.is_exiting_scope()\n    }\n\n    /// Check if the parser is at the same scope level\n    pub fn is_same_level(&self) -> bool {\n        self.current_transition_state.is_same_level()\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current scope depth\n    ///\n    /// Returns the number of active scopes in the hierarchy.\n    /// Root scope has depth 1.\n    pub fn scope_depth(&self) -> usize {\n        self.scope_depth\n    }\n\n    /// Check if we're at the root scope (depth == 1)\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if we're at the root scope, `false` otherwise\n    pub fn is_at_root(&self) -> bool {\n        self.scope_depth == 1\n    }\n\n    /// Check if we're in a nested scope (depth > 1)\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `true` if we're in a nested scope, `false` if at root\n    pub fn is_in_nested_scope(&self) -> bool {\n        self.scope_depth > 1\n    }\n\n    /// Get a reference to the scope stack\n    pub fn scope_stack(&self) -> &ScopeStack {\n        &self.scope_stack\n    }\n\n    /// Get mutable reference to the scope stack\n    pub fn scope_stack_mut(&mut self) -> &mut ScopeStack {\n        &mut self.scope_stack\n    }\n\n    /// Get the current scope from the stack\n    pub fn current_scope(&self) -> Option<&crate::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope> {\n        if self.scope_stack.scopes.is_empty() {\n            None\n        } else {\n            self.scope_stack.scopes.last()\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get parent scope at a specific depth\n    ///\n    /// # Arguments\n    ///\n    /// * `depth_offset` - How many levels up to go (1 = immediate parent, 2 = grandparent, etc.)\n    ///\n    /// # Returns\n    ///\n    /// `Some(&Scope)` if a parent exists at that level, `None` otherwise\n    ///\n    /// # Examples\n    ///\n    /// ```\n    /// let parser = BasicParser::new();\n    /// // If in scope \"a.b.c\", depth_offset=1 returns \"b\" scope, depth_offset=2 returns \"a\" scope\n    /// ```\n    pub fn parent_scope(&self, depth_offset: usize) -> Option<&crate::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope> {\n        if self.scope_stack.scopes.is_empty() || depth_offset == 0 {\n            None\n        } else if depth_offset >= self.scope_stack.scopes.len() {\n            None\n        } else {\n            self.scope_stack.scopes.get(self.scope_stack.scopes.len() - 1 - depth_offset)\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// Get the immediate parent scope (one level up)\n    pub fn immediate_parent_scope(&self) -> Option<&crate::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope> {\n        self.parent_scope(1)\n    }\n\n    /// Get the scope hierarchy path as a string\n    ///\n    /// Returns a dot-separated path representing the scope hierarchy,\n    /// e.g., \"services.web.database\" for a deeply nested scope.\n    pub fn scope_path(&self) -> String {\n        self.scope_stack.get_scope_path()\n    }\n\n    /// Get all scopes in the hierarchy as a slice\n    pub fn scope_hierarchy(&self) -> &[crate::parsers::yaml::scope::Scope] {\n        &self.scope_stack.scopes\n    }\n\n    /// Update scope depth to match the current scope stack state\n    ///\n    /// This should be called after operations that modify the scope stack\n    /// to keep the scope_depth field in sync.\n    fn update_scope_depth(&mut self) {\n        self.scope_depth = self.scope_stack.depth();\n    }\n\n    /// Get all indent transitions from parsing\n    ///\n    /// This requires re-parsing to collect transitions. For more efficient\n    /// access, use the scope stack directly during validation.\n    /// Updates parser state with line classifications and transition tracking.\n    pub fn get_indent_transitions(&mut self, content: &str) -> Vec<crate::parsers::yaml::scope::IndentTransition> {\n        let mut scope_stack = self.scope_stack.clone();\n\n        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n            let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n            // Skip document markers and directives\n            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" || trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n                // Update state to track that we're on an empty line (marker line)\n                self.current_line_type = LineClassification::Empty;\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n            let line_type = classify_line_type(line);\n\n            // Track line type in parser state\n            self.current_line_type = line_type;\n\n            // Only track actual indent changes\n            if indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent() {\n                let has_key = line_type.is_key_bearing();\n                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n\n                // Update transition state to track this indent change\n                let from_indent = scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n                self.current_transition_state.update(from_indent, indent, has_key);\n            }\n        }\n\n        scope_stack.get_indent_transitions().to_vec()\n    }\n\n    /// Get transition counts by type\n    ///\n    /// Returns (enter_scope_count, exit_scope_count, same_level_count)\n    /// Updates parser state with line classifications during counting.\n    pub fn get_transition_counts(&mut self, content: &str) -> (usize, usize, usize) {\n        let transitions = self.get_indent_transitions(content);\n\n        let enter = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_enter_scope()).count();\n        let exit = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_exit_scope()).count();\n        let same = transitions.iter().filter(|t| t.is_same_level()).count();\n\n        (enter, exit, same)\n    }\n\n    /// Detect duplicate keys using scope-aware tracking\n    ///\n    /// This method processes YAML content line-by-line, tracking keys within\n    /// their proper scope contexts. It returns a list of validation errors\n    /// for any duplicate keys found within the same scope.\n    fn detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(&self, content: &str, scope_stack: &mut ScopeStack) -> Vec<ValidationError> {\n        let mut duplicate_errors = Vec::new();\n\n        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n            let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n            // Classify the line type to enable type-specific scope handling\n            let line_type = classify_line_type(line);\n\n            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                {\n                    log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n                }\n                scope_stack.reset();\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Skip YAML directives\n            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n\n            // Track ALL indent changes for detection purposes, regardless of line type\n            // This enables analysis of indent transitions even on blank lines, comments, etc.\n            let indent_changed = indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n            if indent_changed {\n                // Use line classification to determine if this line has a key\n                let has_key = line_type.is_key_bearing();\n                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n            }\n\n            // Handle blank lines and comments with type-specific logic\n            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n            // Comments are also transparent to scope tracking since YAML parsers ignore them.\n            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                // Update last_indent tracking so the next line can detect indent changes\n                // but don't trigger any scope transitions\n                if indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent() {\n                    scope_stack.set_last_indent(indent);\n                }\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Type-specific handling: indent-only lines (no key token)\n            // These lines don't trigger scope entry but may trigger scope exit on indent decrease\n            if !line_type.is_key_bearing() {\n                // Indent-only line - handle scope exit if indent decreased\n                if indent < scope_stack.current_indent() {\n                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n                }\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n                Ordering::Greater => {\n                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else {\n                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                            }\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    } else {\n                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n                        // - A scalar value continuation\n                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                        {\n                            log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Ordering::Less => {\n                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n                    // Check if we're transitioning to a sequence item at same level as parent\n                    // In this case, we should NOT exit the parent scope\n                    let trimmed = line.trim();\n                    let is_sequence_item_at_parent_level = trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") &&\n                        scope_stack.current_indent() == indent + scope_stack.base_indent();\n\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}', is_sequence_item_at_parent_level={}\",\n                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path, is_sequence_item_at_parent_level);\n                    }\n\n                    if !is_sequence_item_at_parent_level {\n                        scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n                    }\n\n                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Edge case: indent decreased but no key - we've already exited to the right scope\n                }\n                Ordering::Equal => {\n                    // Same scope - check for keys\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Scope exit for sibling: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                    // Edge case: same indent but no key - just continue, no scope change needed\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                        duplicate_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                            dup_err.message()\n                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    // Sequence item without a key context\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        log_debug!(\"[detect_duplicate] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n                            indent, line_num_1index);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        duplicate_errors\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Default for BasicParser {\n    fn default() -> Self {\n        Self::new()\n    }\n}\n\nimpl Parser for BasicParser {\n    fn parse_str(&self, content: &str) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Create a new scope stack for this parsing operation\n        let mut scope_stack = ScopeStack::new(2);\n        let mut parse_errors = Vec::new();\n\n        // Parse line by line with scope-aware key tracking\n        for (line_num, line) in content.lines().enumerate() {\n            let line_num_1index = line_num + 1;\n            let trimmed = line.trim();\n\n            // Classify the line type to enable type-specific scope handling\n            let line_type = classify_line_type(line);\n\n            // Handle document markers - reset scope tracking\n            if trimmed == \"---\" || trimmed == \"...\" {\n                #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                {\n                    log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Document marker '{}' at line={}, resetting scope stack\", trimmed, line_num_1index);\n                }\n                scope_stack.reset();\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Skip YAML directives\n            if trimmed.starts_with('%') {\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            let indent = calculate_indentation(line);\n\n            // Track ALL indent changes for detection purposes, regardless of line type\n            // This enables analysis of indent transitions even on blank lines, comments, etc.\n            let indent_changed = indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent();\n            if indent_changed {\n                // Use line classification to determine if this line has a key\n                let has_key = line_type.is_key_bearing();\n                scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, has_key, line);\n            }\n\n            // Handle blank lines and comments with type-specific logic\n            // Blank lines should be transparent to scope tracking - they don't trigger\n            // any scope transitions. The next content line will handle any needed scope changes.\n            // Comments are also transparent to scope tracking since YAML parsers ignore them.\n            if trimmed.is_empty() || trimmed.starts_with('#') {\n                // Update last_indent tracking so the next line can detect indent changes\n                // but don't trigger any scope transitions\n                if indent != scope_stack.get_last_indent() {\n                    scope_stack.set_last_indent(indent);\n                }\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Type-specific handling: indent-only lines (no key token)\n            // These lines don't trigger scope entry but may trigger scope exit on indent decrease\n            if !line_type.is_key_bearing() {\n                // Indent-only line - handle scope exit if indent decreased\n                if indent < scope_stack.current_indent() {\n                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n                }\n                continue;\n            }\n\n            // Handle scope transitions based on indentation changes\n            use std::cmp::Ordering;\n            match indent.cmp(&scope_stack.current_indent()) {\n                Ordering::Greater => {\n                    // Indent increased - entering deeper scope\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // Add the parent key to current scope\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            // Enter new scope for the parent key's nested content\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else {\n                            // Indent increased but not a parent key - this is an inline scalar at deeper indent\n                            // Just add the key to current scope, don't create a new scope\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Inline scalar: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, in_sequence={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                            }\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    } else {\n                        // Indent increased but no key context found - this is likely:\n                        // - A sequence item continuation (already handled by sequence scope logic)\n                        // - A scalar value continuation\n                        // - Don't enter a scope for non-key lines\n                        #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                        {\n                            log_debug!(\"[parse_str] No key context: line={}, indent={}, in_sequence={}, skipping scope entry\",\n                                line_num_1index, indent, scope_stack.in_sequence_context());\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Ordering::Less => {\n                    // Indent decreased - exit to parent scope\n                    // Record the indent transition with key\n                    scope_stack.record_indent_transition(line_num_1index, indent, true, line);\n\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Scope exit: from_indent={}, to_indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n                            scope_stack.current_indent(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n\n                    // After exiting, check if this line has a key\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a new parent key at this level\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n                Ordering::Equal => {\n                    // Same scope - check for keys\n                    if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                        if ctx.is_parent_key() {\n                            // This is a sibling parent key at same indent level\n                            // Exit and re-enter scope for the sibling\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                let current_path = scope_stack.get_scope_path();\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Scope exit for sibling: key='{}', indent={}, line={}, current_scope='{}'\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index, current_path);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent);\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                            #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                            {\n                                log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Re-entering scope: type=Mapping, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                                    ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                            }\n                            scope_stack.enter_scope(\n                                indent + scope_stack.base_indent(),\n                                line_num_1index,\n                                Some(ctx.key_name().to_string())\n                            );\n                        } else if ctx.is_inline_scalar() {\n                            if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                                parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                                    format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                                    dup_err.message()\n                                ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                }\n            }\n\n            // Handle sequence items with their own scopes\n            if trimmed.starts_with(\"- \") {\n                // When starting a new sequence item, exit any existing sequence scope at the same indent\n                if scope_stack.current_indent() == indent && scope_stack.in_sequence_context() {\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Exiting previous sequence scope before entering new one at indent={}, line={}\",\n                            indent, line_num_1index);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.exit_to_scope(indent.saturating_sub(1));\n                }\n\n                if let Some(ctx) = extract_key_context(line) {\n                    // Enter a sequence scope for this item\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Sequence, key='{}', indent={}, line={}\",\n                            ctx.key_name(), indent, line_num_1index);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n                    // Add the key to the sequence scope\n                    if let Err(dup_err) = scope_stack.add_key(ctx.key_name(), line_num_1index) {\n                        parse_errors.push(ValidationError::new(\n                            format!(\"line_{}\", line_num_1index),\n                            dup_err.message()\n                        ).with_line(line_num_1index));\n                    }\n                } else {\n                    // Sequence item without a key context\n                    #[cfg(debug_assertions)]\n                    {\n                        log_debug!(\"[parse_str] Entering scope: type=Sequence (no key), indent={}, line={}\",\n                            indent, line_num_1index);\n                    }\n                    scope_stack.enter_sequence_scope(indent, line_num_1index);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n\n        // Return parse result using serde_yaml for the actual value\n        match serde_yaml::from_str::<serde_yaml::Value>(content) {\n            Ok(value) => {\n                // Note: parse_errors from scope tracking are currently informational\n                // They could be added as warnings in a future enhancement\n                ParseResult::success(value)\n            }\n            Err(err) => {\n                // Add any duplicate key errors we found to the parse error\n                let error_msg = if parse_errors.is_empty() {\n                    err.to_string()\n                } else {\n                    format!(\"{}; duplicate key errors: {}\", err, parse_errors.iter()\n                        .map(|e| e.message.clone()).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(\"; \"))\n                };\n                ParseResult::failure(ParseError::syntax(error_msg))\n            }\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn parse_bytes(&self, content: &[u8]) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Convert bytes to string and parse\n        match std::str::from_utf8(content) {\n            Ok(utf8_content) => self.parse_str(utf8_content),\n            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Invalid UTF-8: {}\", err))),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn parse_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ParseResult<serde_yaml::Value> {\n        // Read file content\n        match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n            Ok(content) => self.parse_str(&content),\n            Err(err) => ParseResult::failure(ParseError::io(format!(\"Failed to read file {}: {}\", path.display(), err))),\n        }\n    }\n\n    fn validate_str(&self, content: &str) -> ValidationResult {\n        // Create syntax validator based on parser mode\n        let validator = if self.config.is_strict() {\n            SyntaxValidator::strict()\n        } else {\n            SyntaxValidator::lenient()\n        };\n\n        // Run syntax validation\n        let mut result = validator.validate(content);\n\n        // Run scope-aware duplicate key detection (when duplicates are not allowed)\n        if !self.config.allow_duplicates {\n            let mut scope_stack = self.scope_stack.clone();\n            let duplicate_errors = self.detect_duplicate_keys_with_scope(content, &mut scope_stack);\n            if !duplicate_errors.is_empty() {\n                result.valid = false;\n                result.errors.extend(duplicate_errors);\n            }\n        }\n\n        // If no errors from basic validation, run enhanced detection\n        if result.is_valid() {\n            let mut detector = SyntaxDetector::new();\n            let detector_result = detector.detect_to_validation_result(content);\n\n            // Merge errors from detector\n            if !detector_result.is_valid() {\n                result.valid = false;\n                result.errors.extend(detector_result.errors);\n            }\n        }\n\n        result\n    }\n\n    fn validate_file(&self, path: &std::path::Path) -> ValidationResult {\n        // Read file content\n        let content = match std::fs::read_to_string(path) {\n            Ok(content) => content,\n            Err(err) => {\n                return ValidationResult {\n                    valid: false,\n                    errors: vec![ValidationError::new(\n                        path.display().to_string(),\n                        format!(\"failed to read file: {}\", err)\n                    )],\n                    warnings: Vec::new(),\n                };\n            }\n        };\n\n        // Validate the content\n        self.validate_str(&content)\n    }\n\n    fn config(&self) -> &ParserConfig {\n        &self.config\n    }\n\n    fn with_config(self, config: ParserConfig) -> Self\n    where\n        Self: Sized,\n    {\n        Self {\n            config,\n            scope_stack: ScopeStack::new(2), // Standard 2-space YAML indentation\n            current_line_type: LineClassification::Empty,\n            current_transition_state: IndentTransitionState::new(),\n            scope_depth: 1, // Root scope is always present\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// ============================================================================\n// Integration Tests for Scope Tracking\n// ============================================================================\n\n#[cfg(test)]\nmod integration_tests {\n    use super::*;\n\n    /// Test parse_str with nested YAML structures\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_str_with_nested_yaml() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n        let yaml = r#\"\nservices:\n  web:\n    host: localhost\n    port: 8080\n  database:\n    host: db.example.com\n    port: 5432\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should parse valid nested YAML successfully\");\n\n        // Verify we can access the parsed structure\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        assert!(value.is_mapping());\n        assert!(value.get(\"services\").is_some());\n    }\n\n    /// Test parse_str with deeply nested YAML\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_str_with_deeply_nested_yaml() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n        let yaml = r#\"\napplication:\n  server:\n    config:\n      timeouts:\n        connect: 30\n        read: 60\n    logging:\n      level: info\n  database:\n    connection:\n      pool:\n        max: 10\n        min: 2\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Should parse deeply nested YAML successfully\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that same key in different scopes passes\n    #[test]\n    fn test_same_key_different_scopes_passes() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        // This YAML has 'host' and 'port' in different scopes\n        let yaml = r#\"\nservices:\n  web:\n    host: localhost\n    port: 8080\n  database:\n    host: db.example.com\n    port: 5432\n  cache:\n    host: redis.example.com\n    port: 6379\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Same key in different scopes should be allowed\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        let services = &value[\"services\"];\n        assert_eq!(services[\"web\"][\"host\"], \"localhost\");\n        assert_eq!(services[\"database\"][\"host\"], \"db.example.com\");\n        assert_eq!(services[\"cache\"][\"host\"], \"redis.example.com\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test that duplicate key in same scope is detected by strict parser\n    #[test]\n    fn test_duplicate_key_same_scope_detected() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::strict(); // Strict parser doesn't allow duplicates\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\nconfig:\n  server:\n    host: localhost\n    host: duplicate-value\n    port: 8080\n\"#;\n\n        // The parse_str itself may succeed (serde_yaml's behavior), but validation should detect the duplicate\n        let validation_result = parser.validate_str(yaml);\n        assert!(!validation_result.is_valid(), \"Validation should detect duplicate keys\");\n\n        // Check that error message mentions duplicate key\n        let error_messages: Vec<String> = validation_result.errors\n            .iter()\n            .map(|e| e.message.clone())\n            .collect();\n        assert!(error_messages.iter().any(|msg| msg.contains(\"duplicate\") || msg.contains(\"host\")));\n    }\n\n    /// Test parse_str with sequence items having same keys\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_str_sequence_items_same_keys() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\nitems:\n  - name: item1\n    value: 100\n  - name: item2\n    value: 200\n  - name: item3\n    value: 300\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Sequence items with same keys should be allowed\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        let items = value[\"items\"].as_sequence().unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(items.len(), 3);\n    }\n\n    /// Test parse_str with mixed mapping and sequence scopes\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_str_mixed_mapping_sequence() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\nservices:\n  web:\n    endpoints:\n      - path: /api\n        method: GET\n      - path: /health\n        method: GET\n    config:\n      timeout: 30\n  database:\n    endpoints:\n      - path: /query\n        method: POST\n    config:\n      pool_size: 10\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Mixed mapping and sequence structures should parse successfully\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test parse_str with multiple indent transitions\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_str_multiple_indent_transitions() {\n        let parser = BasicParser::new();\n\n        let yaml = r#\"\nlevel1_a:\n  level2_a:\n    level3:\n      value: 1\n  level2_b:\n    level3:\n      value: 2\nlevel1_b:\n  level2:\n    level3:\n      value: 3\n\"#;\n\n        let result = parser.parse_str(yaml);\n        assert!(result.is_success(), \"Multiple indent transitions should be handled correctly\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test parse_str with inline scalars (no scope creation)\n    #[test]\n    fn test_parse_str_inline_scalars() {\n        let parser = scalars should parse successfully\");\n\n        let value = result.unwrap();\n        assert_eq!(value[\"name\"], \"test\");\n        assert_eq!(value[\"version\"], \"1.0\");\n    }\n\n    /// Test
